description_long: This plant pot is constructed from irregularly shaped, interlocking stone pieces, giving it a rugged, hand-built appearance reminiscent of traditional stone masonry. The stones vary in size and color, ranging from light tan to darker brown, with visible seams and slight gaps between them. The pot has a cylindrical form with a slightly uneven rim, adding to its rustic charm. Subtle hints of moss or lichen are visible in some of the crevices, suggesting outdoor exposure and natural weathering. The interior appears hollow and deep enough to accommodate a medium-sized plant, while the thick stone walls provide substantial durability. The overall texture is rough and tactile, with a matte, unpolished finish that enhances its naturalistic look.
description: A rugged, cylindrical plant pot made of interlocking, irregular stone pieces with a rough, weathered texture and earthy tones.
description_view_0: The front view shows a prominent arrangement of large, flat stones with visible seams and a slightly uneven rim.
description_view_1: This view highlights a more vertical alignment of stones and a deeper crevice near the top edge.
description_view_2: Here, the pot displays a mix of medium and small stones, with some greenish moss visible in the gaps.
description_view_3: This side reveals a denser pattern of smaller stones and a more pronounced roughness along the rim.
attribute_placement_context: ideal for rustic garden paths or park walkways
